title: e2e: use isRootless() instead of rootless.IsRootless()
body:
Unify the functions used to detect rootless to "isRootless()".
This function can detect to join the user namespace by mistake.

Signed-off-by: Toshiki Sonoda <sonoda.toshiki@fujitsu.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
> Doesn't that mean that the ginkgo processes run inside the user NS?
> Why is that the case?

Because the podman user namespace was already created and importing pkg/rootless will automatically join it by default. All ginkgo test must be run with the remote build tag.
read https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16322 for more details
 
In short the tests should never join the userns. So this patch fixes the symptom but not the cause.
@sstosh how are you running the tests?

Thanks for your reply.
I verified that `os.Geteuid()` returns '1000' and a test is successed (skipped).
<details>
<summary>os.Geteuid() returns '1000' and a test is successed</summary>

```
$ PODMAN_BINARY=$(which podman) PODMAN_REMOTE_BINARY=$(which podman-remote) GOPATH=~/go ginkgo -tags "remote" -v test/e2e/.

Podman pod create
  podman pod create --sysctl test
  /home/test/podman-sstosh/test/e2e/pod_create_test.go:1044
1000
Running: /usr/local/bin/podman --storage-opt vfs.imagestore=/tmp/podman/imagecachedir --root /tmp/podman_test2884809651/root --runroot /tmp/podman_test2884809651/runroot --runtime crun --conmon /usr/bin/conmon --network-config-dir /home/test/.config/cni/net.d --network-backend cni --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /tmp/podman_test2884809651 --events-backend file --storage-driver vfs pod rm -fa -t 0
output:
Running: /usr/local/bin/podman --storage-opt vfs.imagestore=/tmp/podman/imagecachedir --root /tmp/podman_test2884809651/root --runroot /tmp/podman_test2884809651/runroot --runtime crun --conmon /usr/bin/conmon --network-config-dir /home/test/.config/cni/net.d --network-backend cni --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /tmp/podman_test2884809651 --events-backend file --storage-driver vfs rm -fa -t 0
output:

S [SKIPPING] [0.096 seconds]
Podman pod create
/home/test/podman-sstosh/test/e2e/pod_create_test.go:23
  podman pod create --sysctl test [It]
  /home/test/podman-sstosh/test/e2e/pod_create_test.go:1044

  [rootless]: Network sysctls are not available root rootless
```
</details>

I changed a commit message.
In addition, I added a description of `-tags "remote"` to `test/README.md`.

I think in this case it makes more sense to check the euid as we did before so we are sure we didn't join the user namespace.  Now if we join the user namespace by mistake, we won't notice it since `rootless.IsRootless()` would detect it and return `true`

I have the same opinion as @giuseppe does.
I unified the function with `isRootless()` instead of `rootless.IsRootless()`.

LGTM and happy green test buttons.
WOuld like a head nod from @giuseppe before merging.
